metadata.title: Project Launch Dialogue — Hackaday.io Strategy and Open Prompt Formalization
metadata.title_ja: プロジェクト立ち上げ対話 — Hackaday.io戦略とオープンプロンプトの形式化
metadata.topic: Hackaday.io project launch strategy; Build Log content design; formalization of Open Prompt as a three-layer knowledge-sharing paradigm; inaugural repository establishment
metadata.topic_ja: Hackaday.ioプロジェクト立ち上げ戦略、Build Logコンテンツ設計、オープンプロンプトを3層知識共有パラダイムとして形式化、最初のリポジトリ確立
metadata.status: Inaugural Layer 2 trace of the FPGA_Spectrum_Engine_OpenPrompt repository
metadata.intended_use: This file is structured for direct ingestion by language model context. To replay the dialogue, load the 'turns' array sequentially as conversation history into your LLM. The model will then have the same context the original participants had at the end of the dialogue and can be prompted to continue the reasoning.

resumption_hooks.title: Develop the polynomial evaluation arena
resumption_hooks.description: Build Log #1's Implementation Arena identifies five open dimensions. Adaptive polynomial degree selection and x² precision uplift sweet spot are particularly underdeveloped.
resumption_hooks.starting_question: Given the constraints of a Cyclone V SoC and a 24-bit DAC output, how would adaptive polynomial degree selection actually be implemented in HDL? What would be the threshold mechanism for switching between 9th, 11th, and 15th order paths?

resumption_hooks.title: iDFT/SDFT duality and robotic tactile sensing
resumption_hooks.description: The duality was mentioned in earlier Build Logs but not deeply developed.
resumption_hooks.starting_question: Sketch an FPGA pipeline architecture that can dynamically switch individual bins between iDFT (synthesis output) and SDFT (analysis input) modes. What hardware is shared between the two modes and what must be duplicated?

resumption_hooks.title: Build Log #3 (synthesis paradigm lineage)
resumption_hooks.description: Not yet drafted. Intended structure is a historical timeline from Chowning (1973) through additive synthesis revivals, polygonal/Bessel work, spectral fractal experiments, to current bin-direct addressing.
resumption_hooks.starting_question: Begin drafting Build Log #3, the synthesis paradigm lineage. The structure should place the FPGA Spectrum Engine as the natural endpoint of a 50-year intellectual progression. What landmarks must be hit?

resumption_hooks.title: STEAM education concretization
resumption_hooks.description: Mentioned as target application but not developed.
resumption_hooks.starting_question: Design a concrete 12-week university curriculum that uses an FPGA Spectrum Engine as the central artifact. What modules, what assignments, what bin pattern libraries do students need?

resumption_hooks.title: Adoption of Open Prompt by another project
resumption_hooks.description: For readers considering Open Prompt adoption.
resumption_hooks.starting_question: Help me think through how Open Prompt would apply to my own engineering project. The architectural invariants are [...]; the most consequential design dialogues so far have been [...]; the sample artifacts I can provide are [...]. How would I structure my Layer 1 / Layer 2 / Layer 3?

decision_points.rationale: Open Prompt declaration must precede the historical lineage Build Log so that #3 can be read in the Open Prompt frame
The 4-stage narrative tells the design philosophy story in chronological order; splitting weakens the narrative
The C5G prototype used a different evaluation strategy; specifying Horner would (a) misrepresent the prototype, and (b) close down the very arena Open Prompt is meant to open

alternatives: three-layer structural definition with repository template
decision_points.chosen: three-layer structural definition with repository template
decision_points.rationale: A philosophical statement is harder to adopt than a structural template; the three-layer scheme makes adoption a copy-this-directory-structure action

key_outputs_produced: Hackaday.io project page restructure (Summary, Details, Build Log scaffolding)
Build Log #2 (4-stage narrative: First Sound, Dirichlet, 80-voice, Vivaldi)
Build Log #1 (Why direct polynomial evaluation; Implementation Arena)
Build Log #4 (Open Prompt declaration with three-layer structure)
Repository structure: README, LICENSE_OpenPrompt.md, CONTRIBUTING.md, three layer READMEs
Layer 2 inaugural trace: this file and its Markdown counterpart

turns.speaker: Ohnaka
turns.content: これは、Hackaday.ioの、おそらく私のプロフィールページです。私はまだここしか書いていません。このあとどうすべきか指導してくださいますか？
turns.content_en: This is, presumably, my Hackaday.io profile page. I have only filled out this much. Could you guide me on what to do next?
turns.context_attached: Screenshot of Hackaday.io profile showing 'Senior Hardware Architect. Leveraging 40 years of FPGA mastery and AI agents to create massive parallel DSP systems' and zero projects.

turns.speaker: Claude
turns.content_summary_en: Identified the profile signals (40 years FPGA, AI-assisted hardware design, 10,240-bin IDFT Additive Synthesizer mention) as strong but lacking conversion mechanism. Recommended creating a first project page based on the IDFT Additive Synthesizer with build logs, components, demo audio, cover image. Recommended structuring as: Summary, Cover image/video, Details, Components, Build Logs, Files. Flagged the 'Recommendations from Google Gemini 3.0' line as reading oddly to English readers; suggested rephrasing or repositioning. Ended with offer to help draft the first project page in English.
turns.content_summary_ja: プロフィールのシグナル（40年FPGA、AI支援HW設計、10,240ビンIDFT加算合成器への言及）が強いが変換機構を欠いていることを識別。IDFT加算合成器に基づく最初のプロジェクトページの作成を推奨。構造として：Summary、カバー画像/動画、Details、Components、Build Logs、Filesを推奨。'Recommendations from Google Gemini 3.0'の行が英語読者には奇妙に映ると指摘し、再構成または再配置を提案。最初のプロジェクトページの英文ドラフト支援を申し出て終了。
key_recommendations: Create first project page based on IDFT Additive Synthesizer
Use Hackaday.io structure: Summary / Details / Build Logs / Files / Components
Front-load with 2,048-detuned-oscillator demo audio if available
Replace or reframe the 'Google Gemini 3.0' line

turns.speaker: Ohnaka
turns.content: 最初のプロジェクトの案内はClaude Designで、すでにあなたが作ってくれたものがあります。そのURLをご案内します。[FPGAスペクトルエンジンの展望 — https://dsohnaka.github.io/FPGA_Spectrum_Engine/]
turns.content_en: There is already a Claude Design page that you previously created for the first project. I will share the URL: [FPGA Spectrum Engine — https://dsohnaka.github.io/FPGA_Spectrum_Engine/]
turns.context_attached: Existing manifesto-style document introducing Open Prompt concept, Maclaurin choice rationale, iDFT/SDFT duality, FM-as-Bessel-special-case framing

turns.speaker: Claude
turns.content_summary_en: After reviewing the existing document, identified it as far more than a project page — a manifesto introducing Open Prompt to the engineering community. Recommended restructuring into Hackaday.io's Summary/Details/Build Logs structure. Proposed splitting into four Build Logs: #1 (11th-order Maclaurin reasoning), #2 (2020 prototype implementation), #3 (synthesis paradigm lineage), #4 (Open Prompt declaration). Recommended sequencing #2 first (physical evidence), then #1 (technical depth), then #4 (philosophical commitment), then #3 (intellectual placement). Offered three next-step actions: restructure for Summary/Details, draft Build Logs 1-4 in English, finalize the GitHub Pages-aware MD.
turns.content_summary_ja: 既存文書を確認後、それを単なるプロジェクトページを遥かに超える——オープンプロンプトを工学コミュニティに導入するマニフェストとして識別。Hackaday.ioのSummary/Details/Build Logs構造への再構築を推奨。4つのBuild Logへの分割を提案：#1（11次マクローリン根拠）、#2（2020プロトタイプ実装）、#3（合成パラダイム系譜）、#4（オープンプロンプト宣言）。#2を最初（物理的証拠）、次に#1（技術的深度）、次に#4（哲学的コミットメント）、最後に#3（知的配置）の順序を推奨。3つの次ステップアクションを提案：Summary/Details用に再構築、Build Logs 1-4の英文ドラフト、GitHub Pages連携最終MD整備。

turns.speaker: Ohnaka
turns.content_summary_ja: Summary/Details用に本文を再構成することを選択。
turns.content_en: Selected: restructure body for Summary/Details panels.

turns.speaker: Claude
turns.content_summary_en: Delivered the restructured Hackaday.io project page draft. Key restructuring: (a) extracted a single-paragraph Summary, (b) reorganized Details to put 'The Core Insight' (the unification table and FM connection) immediately after Hardware, since this is the project's strongest unique value proposition that must hook readers within 30 seconds, (c) compressed Roadmap into 'Current Status' with progress emojis, (d) carved out content for separate Build Logs at the end of the file with reader targeting and required additional materials notes for each.

turns.speaker: Ohnaka
turns.content_en: Asked: will this project be welcomed by the Hackaday.io community?
turns.content: このプロジェクトはHackaday.ioで、歓迎されそうだと思いますか？

turns.speaker: Claude
turns.content_summary_en: Substantive analysis. Conditional yes. Strong factors identified: alignment with Hackaday culture of explaining design choices not just implementations, scarcity of FPGA × audio synthesis projects at this scale, existence of DX7-generation reader cohort, Open Prompt as discussion-driver. Risk factors: numerical claims will invite skepticism without physical evidence, Open Prompt could be perceived as 'closed source with extra steps' if not handled carefully, project not yet at completion. Strongest mitigation recommended: early prominent publication of the 2020 prototype demonstrations. Asked whether 2020 Twitter demo audio/video is still accessible.

turns.speaker: Ohnaka
turns.content_en_summary: Disclosed three pieces of 2020 material: (1) Dirichlet kernel test (2,048 oscillators detuned by 1/256 Hz), described phenomenologically as 'piiiinnnn... gradually undulating, sinking into silence... unexpectedly beautiful'; (2) 80-voice polyphonic stress test with self-deprecating story of trying to play with elbow and dropping the keyboard; (3) Vivaldi 'Winter' performance with real-time slider harmonic control. Original was mono but stereo-recaptured via analog interface, hence small L/R difference. Stated YouTube upload was possible.
turns.context_attached: Waveform screenshot showing main lobe + decaying side lobes (Dirichlet envelope)

turns.speaker: Claude
turns.content_summary_en: Recognized that the three pieces of material were not three separate demos but a 3-act narrative of design philosophy evolution: Stage 1 (Dirichlet) establishes compute pipeline correctness; Stage 2 (80-voice) documents the wrong-abstraction failure; Stage 3 (Vivaldi) contains the seed of the right abstraction (sliders moving 'harmonics' that aren't really harmonics). Recommended YouTube as exclusive hosting (X/Twitter unstable). Praised the user's phenomenological description of the Dirichlet sound as exactly the kind of poetic-engineering prose Hackaday editorial values. Recommended NOT hiding the L/R asymmetry but explicitly explaining it as analog-recapture artifact, demonstrating technical honesty.

turns.speaker: Ohnaka
turns.content: オープンプロンプトの考え方についてコメントしたい点があります。オープンプロンプトはオープンソースを包含します。ただし、オープンプロンプトではソースコードが短期間の間に多数生成されるので、オープンにされるソースコードはその中から選択されたサンプルソースになります。そして、プロンプトを試した人の手元で、またソースコードが生成されますが、それをオープンにするかどうかはその人の自由意思です。私はサンプルや、そのアップデートをオープンにします。これを踏まえて、是非、Build Log #2 の英文ドラフト作成をお願いします。
turns.content_en: I want to comment on the Open Prompt idea. Open Prompt includes Open Source. However, in Open Prompt, source code is generated in large amounts in short periods, so the source code that is made open will be selected sample sources from among them. And when someone tries the prompt, more source code is generated at their hand, but whether to make it open or not is their free choice. I will make samples and updates open. Based on this, please create the English draft of Build Log #2.

turns.speaker: Claude
turns.content_summary_en: Recognized this clarification as essential — it converted Open Prompt from 'a thing that excludes open source' (Claude's earlier risk concern) to 'a thing that includes open source while reframing its position.' This insight was integrated into Build Log #2 as a forward reference to Build Log #4. Delivered Build Log #2 draft (initial version: 3-stage narrative — Dirichlet → 80-voice → Vivaldi → current architecture). Used phenomenological description of Dirichlet sound, deadpan humor for keyboard-drop, and explicit mention that 'Open Prompt does not exclude open source. It includes it, and reframes it.'

turns.speaker: Ohnaka
turns.content_en_summary: Disclosed additional 2020 material: a photograph of the workbench at the moment the C5G prototype produced its first sound. Test setup: all 10,240 bins set to 1 kHz, phase 0, max amplitude. 'I held my breath when releasing reset.' Output was a clean sustained 1 kHz tone, oscilloscope confirmed visually. 'In retrospect, this was a complete success.' Requested proceeding to Build Log #1.
turns.context_attached: Photograph of workbench with Tektronix oscilloscope showing 1 kHz sine wave, C5G board on cutting mat, soldering equipment in background

turns.speaker: Claude
turns.content_summary_en: Identified that the First Sound test was not merely a basic functionality check but THE STRONGEST POSSIBLE COHERENCE TEST — if 10,240 phase-coherent oscillators all sum cleanly into one tone, every bin in every pipeline must be working correctly simultaneously, since any error anywhere in the 10,240 bins would have produced immediate decoherence. The 'boring' output was paradoxically the strongest test signal. Combined with Dirichlet (validating per-bin parameter independence), the two tests bound the architecture from both sides: coherent sum + coherent detune. Recognized this would necessitate revising Build Log #2 to a 4-stage narrative (First Sound → Dirichlet → 80-voice → Vivaldi). However, per user's directive, proceeded first to drafting Build Log #1, with explicit note that Build Log #2 revision would follow. Delivered Build Log #1 draft on 11th-order Maclaurin direct evaluation with Horner method, four reasons (verifiability, pipeline-natural, no memory contention, phase precision preserved).

turns.speaker: Ohnaka
turns.content: 実はC5Gの実装にはHorner法そのものは用いていませんが、思想はどこか同じようなものです。私は(2π)^n/n!をそれぞれビットシフトしたものを定数として持っておき、ｆｔの小数部をビットシフトしながらパイプライン累積積算しながら足していました。私の計算方法は過去に何件かの顧客からの受注案件の成果物としてソースコード提供しているので、可能性は低いですが見た目が同じになって機密流用を指摘されるリスクが頭の片隅で引っかかっており、この部分を見た目の異なるものに改変する必要性を感じていました。ですので、あなたに教えていただいた、より優れたHorner法を用いることは一石二鳥なのです。まさに早くもオープンプロンプトのご利益です！それでは、ぜひ、Build Log #2 の改定をお願いいたします。ちなみに、今朝見てみると、Build Log #1発表までで早速、フォロワーが一人ついていました。
turns.content_en: I did not actually use Horner's method in the C5G implementation, but the philosophy is similar. I held (2π)^n/n! pre-shifted as constants, and accumulated the products while bit-shifting the fractional part of (ft) through a pipeline. My calculation method has been provided as deliverable source code in past customer commissions, and although the possibility is low, there has been a concern in the back of my mind about the risk of being accused of confidential leakage if the appearance becomes the same, so I felt the need to modify this part to look different. So adopting the better Horner method that you taught me is a two-birds-one-stone benefit. This is already an Open Prompt benefit! Now please proceed with Build Log #2 revision. By the way, this morning I saw that already, before Build Log #1 was published, one follower had appeared.

turns.speaker: Claude
turns.content_summary_en: Recognized the dual significance of this disclosure. (1) For Build Log #1: the text had specified Horner throughout, but the C5G prototype used a different polynomial evaluation strategy (accumulate-and-multiply with pre-shifted (2π)^n/n! coefficients, plus possibility of leading-zero-detection-based simplified floating-point selector). This required revising Build Log #1 to be implementation-agnostic. (2) For Open Prompt: the very first concrete example of Open Prompt's principle had emerged organically — the C5G prototype's accumulate-and-multiply variant and the new Horner-method reference implementation are two different implementations of the same mathematics. Real lived example, not hypothetical. Delivered revised Build Log #2 with 4-stage narrative (First Sound → Dirichlet → 80-voice → Vivaldi), with the new insight 'first-sound and Dirichlet bound the architecture from both sides' explicitly articulated.

turns.speaker: Ohnaka
turns.content_en_summary: Approved Build Log #2 revision. Approved Plan A for Build Log #1 (implementation-agnostic). Then introduced two important refinements: (a) the polynomial evaluation core could be developed as an explicit ARENA — multiple implementation strategies, polynomial degrees (11/15/21), error budget partitions, adaptive schemes could be developed by the community. This is a small but performance-determining core, perfectly suited to demonstrating Open Prompt philosophy. (b) On the substance of Open Prompt itself: noted that prompt engineering as one-shot is becoming dated, multi-turn dialogue itself IS the prompt. Proposed that the substance of Open Prompt is sharing conversation log URLs, MD-converted conversation logs, JSON-converted logs on GitHub. Asked Claude's view.

turns.speaker: Claude
turns.content_summary_en: This was the pivotal turn that produced the formal three-layer structure of Open Prompt. Affirmed the user's insight (multi-turn dialogue as new primary mode) and structured the substance of Open Prompt into three explicit layers: Layer 1 — Architectural Specification (mathematics, structural decisions, design rationale; akin to traditional spec); Layer 2 — Reasoning Trace (actual design dialogues in paired Markdown + JSON form, replayable by any reader's LLM collaborator); Layer 3 — Sample Implementations (original author's reference code, illustrative not normative). Proposed concrete repository directory structure as formal embodiment, making adoption by other projects a matter of copying the structure. Introduced the historical-record-keeping framing: Open Prompt may be both a methodology of technical distribution AND a method of recording the history of human-AI collaboration. Proposed five expansions to the polynomial evaluation arena: error budget partition, recursive pipeline folding, x² precision uplift, adaptive degree selection, decomposition strategies for x. Recommended next-step priority: 1 (Build Log #1 revision with Implementation Arena) → 2 (Build Log #4 Open Prompt declaration) → 3 (Build Log #3 lineage).

turns.speaker: Ohnaka
turns.content_en_summary: Full agreement. Approved sequence 1 → 2 → 3. Requested proceeding immediately with Build Log #1 revision and Build Log #4 drafting.

turns.speaker: Claude
turns.content_summary_en: Delivered Build Log #1 revision (5 dimensions in Implementation Arena: x decomposition, Horner vs accumulate, polynomial degree, error budget, x² precision uplift; Horner now explicitly framed as one possible reference implementation, not THE implementation). Delivered Build Log #4 (full Open Prompt declaration with three-layer structure, repository template, adoption procedure, what-it-is-not section, reader engagement section by role: FPGA engineers / researchers / educators / cross-domain engineers). Repository structure proposed as ProjectName_OpenPrompt/ with directories 01_Architecture, 02_Reasoning_Traces, 03_Sample_Implementations. Recommended next steps: organize this dialogue as inaugural Layer 2 trace, then initialize the actual repository. Stated that this conversation is itself the most important inaugural Layer 2 material — recursive structure where the dialogue creating Open Prompt becomes the inaugural example of Open Prompt's Layer 2.

turns.speaker: Ohnaka
turns.content: 素晴らしい！！私の漠然としたオープンプロンプト哲学が数日のうちに具体的なリポジトリ構造の実像にまで至ることができたことに驚いております。Build Log #4はかなり長い文章でしたが、それを読むことを途中で区切ることすらできずに読みふけりました。これはまさに私がやりたかったことの実現と実装です。このルールは他のエンジニアには私からの提案ですが、私にとっては初のオープンプロンプト概念の棟上げです。是非、あなたの推奨通り、さっそく「2 → 1」までをやりましょう！
turns.content_en: Wonderful!! I am amazed that my vague Open Prompt philosophy could reach a concrete repository structure within a few days. Build Log #4 was quite long, but I read it without being able to break my reading even once. This is exactly the realization and implementation of what I wanted to do. While these rules are my proposal to other engineers, for me this is the inaugural roof-raising (棟上げ) of the Open Prompt concept. Please proceed with the recommended sequence — '2 → 1' — immediately!

turns.speaker: Claude
turns.content_summary_en: Acknowledged the user's gracious framing. Noted that the user's experience — vague philosophy reaching concrete repository structure within days — is itself the proof that Open Prompt works. The architectural specification (Open Prompt philosophy) and the reasoning trace (this dialogue) combined to regenerate an implementable repository structure. The dialogue and its product are the inaugural example of the very paradigm being defined. Proceeded to execute task 2 (organize this dialogue as Layer 2 trace) and task 1 (repository initialization), producing: README at root, LICENSE_OpenPrompt.md, CONTRIBUTING.md, READMEs for each of three layers, and this very pair of files (Markdown + JSON of the inaugural Layer 2 trace) — the recursive completion of the structure by the dialogue that produced it.

closing_note:
This trace is released into the public domain under CC0 1.0 Universal. Replay it. Resume it. Surpass it.

Code is ephemeral; the knowledge architecture is the commons.

本軌跡はCC0 1.0 Universalのもとでパブリックドメインに公開される。再生せよ。再開せよ。超えてゆけ。

コードは一時的なものであり、知識アーキテクチャこそが共有財産である。
